# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.

Write secure code.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
  DOCKER_DRIVER: overlay2d
  
gitlab-ci:
  stage: test
  image: docker:latest
  services:
    - name: postgresql10/postgresql:alpine3.8
      env:
        POSTGRES_USER: gitlab
        POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password
  
  before_script:
    # Install PostgreSQL client and other tools needed for the tests, if necessary...
    
  script:
    - echo "Running unit tests..."
    - python manage.py test --noinput || (sleep 10; false) &
      wait $${!}   ;# Waiting indefinitely until all background jobs are done or fail due to timeout, then exit with the last status received by one of our subshells...     # ...or at least try again later. This is a workaround for https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/23610
  
  artifacts:
    reports:
      cobertura_coverage: coverage.xml
      
  allow_failure: false # Optional, if you want to fail the pipeline in case of test failures...    
                       # ...you can set it true here and GitLab will stop running jobs that failed during this stage  
    paths:
      - cobertura_coverage/htmlcov/*.html  # Paths where coverage reports are stored, replace with your actual path if needed!      
        
gitlab-ci:
  image: docker:latest
  stage: deploy
  before_script:
     # Install Docker and other tools necessary for the deployment...  
      - echo $DOCKERHUBPASS | docker login --username gitlabdocker --password-stdin   
      
  script:       
    - 'echo "$CI_PIPELINE_ID" >> /tmp/pipeline.log' # Logging pipeline ID for reference...  
      . ./scripts/* && python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel upload     --no-input || (sleep 10; false) & wait $${!}    ;# Uploading source distribution and wheel to PyPI, if necessary ...       # Wait until all background jobs are done or fail due timeout
  
notify: "mailto:your.email@example.com"     # Optional - If you want notifications on build status change...  You can use any email address here!    Notify will send a notification to this mail with the details of each job, including its state and result if it fails or not
